overview: Processes **IDML** (InDesign Markup Language) documents, the XML-based format introduced in Adobe InDesign CS4 for representing InDesign content. IDML is used in several InDesign and InCopy file types. The filter extracts text by spread, processing stories from `<TextFrame>` and `<TextPath>` elements in spread order.

extraction.extractNotes.help: Extracts the content of `<Note>` elements embedded in the IDML document, making them available as translator notes.

extraction.extractMasterSpreads.help: Extracts content from master spreads in addition to normal spreads. Disable this if master spread content does not require translation (e.g., page numbers, decorative elements).

extraction.extractCustomTextVariables.help: Extracts custom text variables defined in the InDesign document for translation.

extraction.extractExternalHyperlinks.help: Extracts external hyperlink URLs for translation. Useful when hyperlinks contain locale-specific URLs that need to be adapted per target language.

extraction.extractHiddenLayers.help: Includes content from hidden layers in extraction. By default, only visible layers are processed.

extraction.extractHiddenPasteboardItems.help: Includes content from hidden pasteboard items in extraction.

extraction.extractHyperlinkTextSourcesIn.help:
Controls how hyperlink text sources are represented:
- **Enabled**: hyperlink text sources are extracted inline within the surrounding text
- **Disabled**: they are represented as separate referencing groups of textual units

extraction.extractIndexTopics.help: Extracts index topic entries for translation.

extraction.extractBreaksInline.help: Extracts break elements inline within the text flow rather than treating them as segment boundaries.

extraction.untagXmlStructures.help: Skips embedded XML structural information when extracting translatable content. This prevents XML structure tags from appearing as inline codes in the extracted text.

extraction.skipDiscretionaryHyphens.help: Skips discretionary (soft) hyphens during extraction. Discretionary hyphens are optional line-break hints inserted by InDesign.

extraction.specialCharacterPattern.help: Matches special characters that should be treated as inline codes rather than translatable content. The default pattern covers various Unicode special characters including zero-width spaces, zero-width non-joiners, soft hyphens, non-breaking hyphens, and BOM characters.
extraction.specialCharacterPattern.values: A pipe-delimited (`|`) pattern of Unicode characters. Default: `" | | | | | | | | | |\u200B|\u200C|\u00AD|\u2011|\uFEFF"`

extraction.maxAttributeSize.help: Sets the size limit for the XML attribute buffer. Increase this if processing IDML files with very large embedded attributes (e.g., heavy graphics data in attributes).
extraction.maxAttributeSize.values: Size in bytes. Default is 4194304 (4 MB = 4 × 1024 × 1024).

formatting.ignoreCharacterKerning.help:
Ignores kerning differences between adjacent characters when determining formatting runs. This reduces the number of inline codes by merging runs that differ only in kerning values.

When enabled, use the **min** and **max** threshold fields to define the range of kerning values to ignore.

formatting.kerningMin.help: Minimum kerning value threshold. Kerning differences below this value are ignored.
formatting.kerningMin.values: Numeric string representing a kerning value.

formatting.kerningMax.help: Maximum kerning value threshold. Kerning differences above this value are ignored.
formatting.kerningMax.values: Numeric string representing a kerning value.

formatting.ignoreCharacterTracking.help:
Ignores tracking (letter-spacing) differences when determining formatting runs. Reduces inline code clutter from minor tracking variations.

When enabled, use the **min** and **max** threshold fields to define the range of tracking values to ignore.

formatting.trackingMin.values: Numeric string representing a tracking value.

formatting.trackingMax.values: Numeric string representing a tracking value.

formatting.ignoreCharacterLeading.help:
Ignores leading (line-spacing) differences when determining formatting runs. Useful when InDesign applies per-character leading overrides that create unnecessary inline codes.

When enabled, use the **min** and **max** threshold fields to define the range of leading values to ignore.

formatting.leadingMin.values: Numeric string representing a leading value.

formatting.leadingMax.values: Numeric string representing a leading value.

formatting.ignoreCharacterBaselineShift.help:
Ignores baseline shift differences when determining formatting runs. Baseline shift moves characters up or down relative to the baseline — minor shifts (e.g., for superscript/subscript fine-tuning) can create excessive inline codes.

When enabled, use the **min** and **max** threshold fields to define the range of baseline shift values to ignore.

formatting.baselineShiftMin.values: Numeric string representing a baseline shift value.

formatting.baselineShiftMax.values: Numeric string representing a baseline shift value.

extractMathZones.help: Extracts math zones (MathML or equation content) for translation.

excludedStyleConfigurations.help: Enables style-based exclusion. Content with the specified paragraph or character styles is excluded from extraction. Use this to skip non-translatable content such as code listings, product identifiers, or boilerplate styled with specific InDesign styles.

processingNotes: The filter processes text **by spread** — for each spread, it gathers stories used in `<TextFrame>` and `<TextPath>` elements, extracting them in the order they appear in the spread.
Stories embedded inside other stories that are not declared at a spread level are extracted in a special group.
The formatting tolerance parameters (kerning, tracking, leading, baseline shift) reduce inline code clutter by merging adjacent formatting runs that differ only within the specified thresholds.
limitations: Requires Adobe InDesign CS4 or later IDML format — older `.indd` binary files are not supported.
The deprecated `Maximum spread size` parameter (removed in M34) previously allowed skipping large spread files that contained only graphics; current versions process all spreads.
